title: Impulse — 2D Physics Engine
description: A 2D rigid-body physics engine built from scratch in TypeScript — GJK/EPA collision, a unified radius-aware narrowphase for circles, capsules and rounded polygons, and conservative-advancement continuous collision (CCD) so fast bodies never tunnel. The solver is a warm-started sequential-impulse method with split-impulse position correction AND an exact two-point block LCP (Box2D-Lite's four-case analysis) so wide stacks settle flat. It carries a from-scratch buoyancy & fluid-drag subsystem (real Archimedes lift ρ_fluid·A_submerged·(−g) at the centre of buoyancy, submerged area integrated in closed form against the waterline) and spatial queries (AABB region queries + a conservative-advancement convex shape-cast). New in v4 — a machine-builder's toolkit: the Box2D joint family is now complete with a pulley (length-ratio over two ground anchors), a gear joint (couples two revolute/prismatic joints by a ratio — gear trains & rack-and-pinion) and a motor joint (an overpowerable actuator that stalls under load); conveyor surfaces (a per-body tangentSpeed folded into the friction constraint so belts drag bodies up to speed); a radial-impulse / explosion field with distance falloff and static-geometry ray occlusion; and breakable joints that measure their own reaction load and snap past a force/torque budget. New in v5 — a second physics paradigm: from-scratch **soft bodies** via XPBD (extended position-based dynamics). Particles held by compliant distance, bending and area-preservation constraints, advanced with the step-size-independent 'small-steps' substep scheme, collide with every rigid shape through the engine's own core+radius math and exchange momentum two-ways — so a jelly shoves a crate and a falling crate dents a cloth hammock. Builders for pressurised blobs, pinnable cloth/membranes, ropes and lattice solids, a smooth jelly/cloth/rope renderer, and click-to-grab dragging. Nine joint types, a dynamic AABB-tree broadphase, islands & sleeping, and a 34-scene interactive playground backed by a live 123-check correctness suite that re-derives every claim from an analytic reference (soft-body momentum conserved to ~1e-11, fully deterministic).
